title: Triangular Pandemonium
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Triangular Pandemonium'''}} {{Background|file=LEVELDELTAX.png}} __NOTOC__ {{Class|p1=4|p2=5|p3=5|p3-text=Extremely Dangerous Entity|p1-color=|p2-color=|p3-color=}}
{{Blurquote|image=LEVELDELTAX.png|quote-text=
The '''Triangular Pandemonium''' is a dark, unnerving, and enormously dangerous sub-level of '''[[The Delta Mall]]'''. It features several threats to any being that dares to enter this mall, such as holes on the ground and [[#Entity|a cruel entity]]. Only one safe exit from the level has been confirmed so far, so information about it is still somewhat sparse and dubious; most information was obtained through wireless communication devices, such as radios and cell phones.
}}
==Description== {{Blurimage|file=LEVELDELTAX.png|width=550px|caption=The only known image of '''Triangular Pandemonium''', sent via mobile by a wanderer.|hovertext=Something lurks in these dark, silent halls…}} '''Triangular Pandemonium''' is a large and dark triangular outdoor mall, composed of three corridors with two floors, each measuring about 90 meters (about 295 feet) and meeting at their ends, very similar to [[The Delta Mall]]. Despite its resemblance to its parent level, it has many notable differences such as the sky, which is frozen in a starry night look, the destroyed corridors and stores and the non-existent lighting. The three corridors of the level are very silent and dark, with many objects broken and scattered throughout the floor. These objects are predominantly bottles, office supplies, furniture, electronics and toys — anything that can be found in a mall, as if stores had its contents thrown out. There are holes in the walls of the stores that lead to other places and stores of the mall. The state of the park, present in the middle of the main level and supposedly on this one too, is unknown as no one has been there yet, though it is assumed to be destroyed as well. Wanderers stated that the level has some strange properties, just like [[The Delta Mall]]; however, unlike it, none of them are advantageous nor harmless. Firstly, the corridors can change their stores, just like in the parent level; some noted that objects dropped on the floor also change with the aisles, while others said that the halls are sometimes turned, mirrowed or even upside down (It is highly advised that one do not enter an upside down corridor to avoid falling off the level). Also, the '''Triangular Pandemonium''' is devoid of consumables; any food or drink is quite rare and, when found, is not in a good state of consumption. Then, there are some strange particles that appear to be dust, soot and/or ash floating around the level; the inhalation of these particles is not recommended as their origin, composition and effects are unknown. And finally, the hallway lights may sometimes flicker, giving a brief glimpse of light amidst the darkness of the level. The last property is also one of the main hazards of the level: triangular void holes on the ground. They seem to appear randomly throughout the level and are certainly dangerous since they will send wanderers who fall through it to [[The Void]]. But the main danger of '''Triangular Pandemonium''' is its entity, known only as ''The Warden''. It is very dangerous and cruel, as it has been said to hunt down any life forms that enter the level, making the level basically devoid of other entities. This high-danger level is certainly not a place for beginners, especially because no safe exit was discovered yet, so explorers need to know what they're doing. It is very advisable '''not to enter the level''', but if you want anyway, at least some precautions must be followed: *The main problem of void holes is due to the almost eternal blackout of '''Triangular Pandemonium''' that makes them almost invisible; it is highly recommended to take a flashlight, cell phone or any other light-emitting object to the level to ensure you don't fall into these hollows; *While it's nice to have a light source, you need to know when to use it so the entity doesn't see it. Noises can also alert the entity about your location, so it is recommended that you change your shoes for slippers to soften the noise – it is not advised to go barefoot as the chances of you stepping on a noisy or sharp object are high; *It's good to bring food and water so you can survive the inospitable environment and the various inevitable chases of [[#Entity|The Warden]]. ==Entity== {{Blurimage|file=751.png|width=300px|bg-size=300px|caption=A drawing of The Warden. Despite being the best representation of the creature, the absence of a clear photo leaves some information unconfirmed.|hovertext=Fear…}} ''“The Warden”'' is said to be a giant hamster-looking entity with a crude smiling face, gray fur, red glowing eyes and long black claws coming out of its back. Explorers state that this entity is a true monster that is always looking for a living being to pursue and capture. The entity's name, ''“The Warden”'', was given by [[Entity 250|The Hamster]], as he confirmed that this being and the “Strażnik”This is the name he gave to the entity. “Strażnik” means “Warden” in Polish, the language [[Entity 250|The Hamster]] speaks., a creature he talked about during his interviews, are the same despite it's description of the entity's appearance being slightly different. This entity was previously known as Entity 251, but after The Hamster revealing that he created this entity, albeit unintentionally, and that it was once a Hamster from his society like him — also due to many changes to the database —, ''The Warden's'' classification had to be changed. [[File:751Picture.png|thumb|250px|The only photo taken of The Warden to this date, which confirms its red glowing eyes. The photographer was presumably devoured.]] Even when little was known about the level, it was already possible to say with certainty that this entity is its main danger. ''The Warden'' is very silent, moving around the mall just making faint thuds while walking with its claws and a constant low frequency noise, making it difficult to tell if it is nearby; the entity's low frequency noise was recorded and found to be a monotonic sound with a frequency of exactly 900Hz. Even though it moves delicately and silently, the entity is also quite fast, so it takes a bit of effort to be able to outrun its speed, making water a must-have on this level. Last but not least, the silence of '''Triangular Pandemonium''' makes it easier for the entity to locate careless beings in the level. Some who have witnessed the creature attack other explorers have said that it does so by picking them, lifting them up, opening its mouth and devouring them whole; some pointed this can be considered a wardening behavior, such as territorialism. When the entity does this, it is possible to see the inside of its mouth: ironically, it is very white and shiny, has some “weird black shape-shifting marks” — as some have said — and casts a beam of light like a flashlight whenever it opens its mouth after capturing someone. It has also been heard through mobile devices that, while its mouth is open, the entity produces a loud, multi-pitched scream that “appears to be a condensation of many sounds”; most say the sound is shrill and gruff at the same time or simply indescribable. This all shows that ''The Warden'' is not one to mess with, so when on the level, be silent and '''avoid this entity at all costs'''. ==Colonies and Outposts== Because of the harsh nature of '''Triangular Pandemonium''' and its entity, colonies and outposts are impossible to be established. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrance=== There is only one known way of getting into this shady level: finding, collecting and arranging all four dark hamster statues on the tables that are closest to the tree in [[The Delta Mall]]. Though being unadvised by other wanderers and [[Entity 250|The Hamster]] itself, performing these steps will open a black opaque portal on the tree trunk that leads to this level. The portal will disappear from [[The Delta Mall]] shortly after a being crosses it or after a while if nothing crosses it – it is not seen in '''Triangular Pandemonium''', which explains why nobody came back from there through it. After the portal closes, the statues will appear to slowly evaporate, materializing back at their respective stores right after. ===Exits===
{{Audio|file=The_Warden_Rec.mp3|=no|align=center}}
The monotonic sound of ''The Warden'' in the distance.
The only safe way out of the '''Triangular Pandemonium''' was doubtful before its recent confirmation: some claimed to have found a well-hidden white hamster statue in the devastated mall that allows one to exit the level. Since communication was mysteriously cut off whenever people told about it, it was only when some explorers who got on the level a long time ago and talked about the statue via call reappeared that it was possible to state that this is a safe way to exit the level. However, it is important to know that the location of the statue seems to be constantly changing, as explorers' descriptions of where it is differ greatly. Those who returned from the Triangular Pandemonium said that, upon touching the statue, they were transported to a completely white place, which they pointed out as probably being "the inside of the statue", although their subsequent description make this place seem more like [[The Whole]]: They claim to have floated for an unknown amount of time, feeling as if their energy was being drained from them every hour until they passed out. Then, each of them reported waking up on a different level of the Backrooms, such as [[Level 0|The Lobby]], [[Level 54]], and [[The Hub]]. Apparently wanderers are taken to completely random levels, which explains why it takes so long for many to get in touch again, and why most fail: many must've been taken to dangerous or difficult-to-escape levels. There are still other supposed ways to get out, however, they are not safe and '''strongly not recommended''' as exits from the level. These are listed below: *Falling into one of the void holes of the level will lead to [[The Void]]. Many speculate that falling through the ceiling of the mall in an upside down alley will lead to The Void as well, but this has yet to be confirmed; *It is presumed that getting caught by [[#Entity|The Warden]] and entering its mouth will lead you to some sort of the creature's variation of [[The Void]] or [[The Whole]]; some have recounted entering an unknown void. {{Underline|ff000080}}
